customer_anxiety_profile: No anxiety. Christian is a technically sophisticated, self-motivated buyer who has owned solar for years, tracks his usage in a spreadsheet, and is explicitly not cost-sensitive. His motivation is energy independence and battery backup, not bill savings. He will arrive at the appointment with specific technical expectations — treat him as a peer, not a prospect.
unresolved_concerns: Usage spreadsheet not yet received — Holland is waiting on Christian to send it before Thursday. If not received by Tuesday, follow up proactively.

Enphase microinverter availability unconfirmed — Holland told Christian Ion carries Enphase products. This must be verified with Ion's product team before the appointment. If Ion does not carry Enphase, the closer must reframe before the proposal is presented.
Jeanette's presence at the in-home appointment not explicitly confirmed — Christian used 'we' language throughout but Jeanette's Thursday availability was not verified.
sensitive_topics_raised: Mountain View Electric (not Colorado Springs Utilities) — customer noted Mountain View is 'harder to deal with on solar.' Closer should be prepared for net metering complexity with this utility.

Existing Namaste Solar system — customer's prior installer no longer services their ZIP code. Customer may have residual frustration about being abandoned by their original installer; acknowledge the continuity gap.

Inverter swap from string to micro — this is a technical scope item the closer must address. Customer has a specific preference (Enphase microinverters) and will notice if the proposal does not address it.

closer_should_know: Christian is not a cost-payback buyer — he said so explicitly. His ask is specific: add panels to restore net-zero status (now running two EVs and a hot tub), swap string inverters for Enphase microinverters, and add battery backup. The Enphase confirmation Holland gave is the single structural risk: verify Ion's Enphase availability before walking in, and if there is a product mismatch, lead with it transparently rather than letting it surface mid-proposal.
